picks.reasoning: Spain are the reigning European champions and have demonstrated consistent, structured football under their current system. Their head-to-head record against Portugal in competitive fixtures is strong, and at 1.97 this sits squarely in the target range with genuine statistical backing. Portugal without a settled tactical identity post-Ronaldo era present a manageable risk here.

Knights at home at $2.10 is decent value — they've been competitive and home ground matters in the NRL. Dolphins are solid but Knights fans will be loud and I reckon they get up here. Good enough price to have a crack.

Portugal at 4.30 in a World Cup knockout clash against Spain? That's free money sitting on the table! Ronaldo's heir generation has been building to exactly this moment, and Iberian derbies are NEVER straightforward — Spain know it, we know it, and the bookies are sleeping on it. When the upset machine fires in a derby at a World Cup, it pays like a slot jackpot — I'm loading up!
